findings.title: Insufficient Consent Mechanism for GDPR Compliance
findings.description: Current consent mechanisms do not provide granular options for users to opt-in or opt-out of specific data processing activities, violating GDPR requirements.
assumptions: Users need clear options for consent to comply with GDPR.

evidence.excerpt: Current consent mechanisms are insufficient for GDPR compliance.

recommendations: Implement granular consent mechanisms that allow users to opt-in or opt-out of specific data processing activities.

findings.title: GDPR Compliance Risk for EU Market Launch
findings.description: The PersonaLens profiling system requires a full GDPR compliance audit before launch, presenting a critical blocker for the EU market due to legal concerns regarding data processing and retention policies.
assumptions: Compliance with GDPR is mandatory for operating in the EU market.

evidence.excerpt: Conduct a full GDPR compliance audit to address concerns regarding lawful data processing.

recommendations: Conduct a full GDPR compliance audit before launch.

metadata.mitigation: Conduct a full GDPR compliance audit.

findings.title: Data Export Compliance Risk
findings.description: The data export system for advertisers poses a risk due to legal concerns regarding anonymization, which could lead to compliance issues if individual re-identification is possible.
assumptions: Anonymization must be robust to prevent re-identification.

evidence.excerpt: Legal team has flagged that individual re-identification may be possible with enough cohort intersections.

recommendations: Enhance anonymization techniques to ensure compliance.

findings.title: Authentication Weaknesses Detected
findings.description: Weaknesses in authentication mechanisms have been identified, which could lead to unauthorized access to user data.
assumptions: Authentication mechanisms must be robust to prevent unauthorized access.

evidence.excerpt: Detected auth risk keywords in multiple sources.

recommendations: Strengthen authentication mechanisms to prevent unauthorized access.

findings.title: Privacy Risk Due to Lack of Transparency
findings.description: Users have expressed concerns about the lack of transparency regarding data tracking and usage, which could lead to trust issues.
assumptions: Transparency in data handling is crucial for user trust.

evidence.excerpt: Users express a strong desire for transparency regarding what data is being tracked.

recommendations: Implement a transparency dashboard for user data.

summary: Identified 5 risks (2 compliance, 2 privacy, 1 security). 2 flagged as blockers. Risk gate: FAILED (2 blockers, 0 warnings).
